mnemonics.title: BGP Path Selection Order
mnemonics.content: LLANOM (Local Preference > Lowest AS_PATH > Lowest Origin > Lowest MED > eBGP over iBGP > Lowest IGP Metric > Lowest Router ID)

mnemonics.title: BGP Message Types
mnemonics.content: OPEN UPdates NOTIFY KeepAlive - O-U-N-K helps remember: Open Updates Notification Keepalive

acronyms.usage: A collection of routers under single administration sharing common routing policy
The protocol that routes Internet traffic between autonomous systems
Optional attribute to influence incoming traffic from neighboring AS
Attribute to influence outbound traffic selection within AS
BGP peering between routers within same AS
BGP peering between routers in different ASes
BGP router that reflects routes to solve iBGP full mesh issue
BGP table containing all learned routes and attributes

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle with 6 states: IDLE (bottom) → CONNECT → ACTIVE → OPEN SENT → OPEN CONFIRM → ESTABLISHED (top). Show transitions: IDLE→CONNECT on Start, CONNECT→OPEN SENT on TCP success, OPEN CONFIRM→ESTABLISHED on KEEPALIVE, any→IDLE on error/Stop

Stack boxes: Well-Known Mandatory (ORIGIN, AS_PATH, NEXT_HOP) at bottom, Well-Known Discretionary (LOCAL_PREF, ATOMIC_AGGREGATE) middle, Optional Transitive (COMMUNITY, AGGREGATOR) top layer, Optional Non-Transitive (MULTI_EXIT_DISC) top

Draw two clouds: AS 100 (left) and AS 200 (right). Show eBGP at boundary passing routes with NEXT_HOP unchanged but advertised. iBGP inside AS 100 showing routes learned from eBGP are passed but NEXT_HOP preserved (may need next-hop-self)

analogies.analogy: Like a passport stamp showing all countries visited - if you see your own country stamp, you know you're going in circles
Like making a phone call: IDLE (phone off) → CONNECT (dialing) → ACTIVE (ringing) → OPEN SENT (saying Hello) → OPEN CONFIRM (they say Hello back) → ESTABLISHED (conversation starts)
BGP is like international postal service (between countries/AS), while OSPF/RIP are like local mail delivery (within a city/AS)
LOCAL_PREF is like choosing which exit to take when leaving your house (internal decision), MED is like telling visitors which door to use when approaching your house (external suggestion)
Like a classroom announcement system - instead of every student telling every other student the news, one person (route reflector) tells everyone
